title: Project Description
description: Input specification for the Agent Teams build_team pipeline. Describes the project, its deliverables, tools, and components to generate a complete agent team.

project_name.description: Short, slug-safe project name (e.g., 'MyResearchProject', 'DataPipelineV2'). If absent, inferred from the project directory name.

project_goal.description: 1–3 sentence description of what the project produces and why. Used as {PROJECT_GOAL} throughout all agent files.

deliverables.description: List of primary deliverable types this project produces.

output_format.description: The final output format for compiled deliverables (e.g., 'HTML5', 'PDF', 'Python 3.11 modules', 'LaTeX'). Used as {OUTPUT_FORMAT}.

primary_output_dir.description: Directory where primary authored deliverables are written, relative to project root (e.g., 'src/', 'html/chapters/', 'docs/'). Used as {PRIMARY_OUTPUT_DIR}.

build_output_dir.description: Directory where compiled/converted output artifacts go (e.g., 'build/', 'dist/', 'manuscript/'). Used as {BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}.

figures_dir.description: Directory for diagrams and figures. Used as {FIGURES_DIR}.

existing_project_path.description: Absolute path to an existing project directory on disk. If provided, the analyzer scans it for structure, tools, and potential authority sources.

agents_output_dir.description: Directory where generated agent files will be written. Defaults to '.github/agents/' within the project path.

authority_sources.description: Files or directories that agents must treat as ground truth. Used to build {AUTHORITY_HIERARCHY} and {AUTHORITY_SOURCES_LIST}.

name.description: Human-readable name of this authority source.

path.description: File or directory path, relative to project root or absolute.

scope.description: What types of claims this source is authoritative for.

rank.description: Priority rank in the authority hierarchy (1 = highest).

style_reference.description: Path to the style guide, voice samples, or editorial conventions file. Used as {STYLE_REFERENCE_PATH}.

reference_db_path.description: Path to the bibliography or reference database (BibTeX, CSV, JSON, Markdown). Used as {REFERENCE_DB_PATH}.

reference_key_convention.description: Citation key format convention. Used as {REFERENCE_KEY_CONVENTION}.

tools.description: Languages, frameworks, and tools the project uses.

config_files.description: Config files associated with this tool.

needs_specialist_agent.description: If true, generate a dedicated tool-specific agent for this tool.

docs_url.description: Official documentation URL for this tool (e.g., 'https://nodejs.org/docs/latest-v20.x/api/'). Used as {TOOL_DOCS_URL} in generated agent files.

api_surface.description: Key classes, functions, CLI commands, or APIs this tool exposes that agents need to understand. Multi-line Markdown is supported. Used as {TOOL_API_SURFACE} in generated agent files.

common_patterns.description: Common usage patterns, version-specific gotchas, and anti-patterns for this tool. Multi-line Markdown is supported. Used as {TOOL_COMMON_PATTERNS} in generated agent files.

retrieval_integration.description: Structured contract for retrieval query, maintenance, and trigger behavior.

query_entrypoints.description: Files, symbols, or commands where retrieval lookups are executed.

maintenance_entrypoints.description: Commands, scripts, or functions that refresh or rebuild retrieval state.

trigger_sources.description: Trigger channels used to run retrieval workflows.

source_of_truth.description: Tables/files used to verify retrieval correctness and freshness.

staleness_slo_minutes.description: Maximum allowed retrieval staleness window in minutes.

trigger_contract_version.description: Version identifier for retrieval trigger governance.

style_rules.description: Project-specific style rules in plain English. Used in copilot-instructions.md.

pip_package_name.description: PyPI package name for pip distribution (e.g., 'agentteams'). Supplies the package name used by the module-doc-author/validator agents when they are selected, and enables --doc-check / --doc-update flags. Selection of those agents is driven by packaging/docs signals in the project description (pypi, sphinx, mkdocs, readthedocs, sdist).

doc_site_tool.description: Documentation site generator. When set, module-doc-author produces the corresponding config file.

selected_archetypes.description: Override the auto-detected archetype selection. If absent, the analyzer selects archetypes from the project description.

capabilities.description: Explicit, opt-in capabilities this project wants beyond archetype selection. `research_verification` selects the `research-analyst` archetype, which orchestrates the optional `agentteams[research]` runtime library (real, installable Python — not a design-time-only template) — a project must deliberately choose this, since it adds a real runtime dependency to the generated project, unlike every other archetype selection.

components.description: Workstream components. One workstream expert agent is generated per component.

name.description: Human-readable name of this component (e.g., 'Chapter 1: Introduction').

output_file.description: Path to this component's primary output file, relative to primary_output_dir.

description.description: 1–3 sentences describing what this component produces.

sections.description: List of sections, functions, or sub-units in this component.

sources.description: Known source references (citation keys or file paths) relevant to this component.

cross_refs.description: Other component slugs this component references.

quality_criteria.description: Pass/fail quality criteria the workstream expert will check against in reviews.

tools.description: Tool or agent slugs relevant to this component (e.g., 'PostgreSQL', 'Docker'). Used to link workstream experts to tool specialists/references.

memory_index_extra_dirs.description: Optional consumer-declared extra source directories or glob patterns merged into the durable memory index in addition to the built-in defaults (workSummaries/, docs_src/, references/, CHANGELOG.md, README.md). Each entry is a project-relative string: a directory (recursively scanned for *.md) or a glob (literal expansion). Absolute paths, traversal escapes, and symlinked escapes outside the project root are rejected. See docs_src/api-reference/memory-index.md.

code_index_extra_dirs.description: Optional consumer-declared extra directories or glob patterns of local scripts merged into the code & API index's local-script partition, in addition to the git-tracked source files discovered by default. Each entry is a project-relative string: a directory (recursively scanned for *.py/*.sh) or a glob (literal expansion of *.py/*.sh). Absolute paths, traversal escapes, and symlinked escapes outside the project root are rejected. The code index is a gitignored local cache. See docs_src/api-reference/code-index.md.

conversion_pipeline.description: Command or tool used to convert primary deliverables to output format. Used as {CONVERSION_PIPELINE} in format-converter agent.

mcp_hints.description: Optional consumer-declared hints about external integrations that may warrant an MCP server. Each entry feeds the MCP-suitability detection rubric (agentteams.mcp_detect). Purely advisory: the rubric only recommends; it never auto-provisions a credentialed server. See references/mcp-auto-detection-report.md §5.

integration.description: Name of the external system or capability (e.g., 'analytics-warehouse', 'jira').

stateful.description: True if the integration has an auth/session lifecycle, connection pooling, or caching that materially benefits from a persistent server.

used_by_components.description: Component slugs that need this integration. Reuse across >=2 components is a positive signal.

trust_tier.description: Self-declared trust level (REQUIRED so absence cannot silently default to the permissive case). Any third-party-* value routes the candidate to DEFER_TO_SECURITY_REVIEW.

max_side_effect.description: Most-privileged operation the integration needs. 'destructive' routes the candidate to DEFER_TO_SECURITY_REVIEW.

large_dynamic_surface.description: True if the operation set is large and changes over time. Only a positive signal when commits_lazy_disclosure is also true; otherwise an efficiency anti-pattern (report §4.1).

commits_lazy_disclosure.description: True if the integration commits to lazy/progressive tool disclosure. Required for large_dynamic_surface to count as a positive signal (report §5.1 tiebreaker).

mcp_servers.description: Optional operator-SPECIFIED MCP server definitions to emit for this team (each conforms to mcp-server.schema.json). This is the input for automating *specified* servers (vs. mcp_hints, which only feeds the BUILD/USE/DEFER detection rubric). Purely declarative: entries are copied to the team-manifest's mcp_servers[] and emitted as the INERT .claude/mcp-servers.agentteams.json only when an MCP host-feature token is enabled (claude:mcp or bridge:copilot-vscode-to-claude:mcp). Provisions nothing on its own; credentialed activation remains a separate operator step. See references/mcp-auto-detection-report.md §5.4/§6.

recipe_parameters.description: Optional Goose recipe parameters (Phase-4a goose-native). Declares typed runtime inputs for the generated Goose team: copied to the team-manifest and, for --framework goose, emitted as a `parameters:` block on the ORCHESTRATOR recipe (referenced via {{ key }}) so it accepts `goose run --recipe orchestrator.yaml --params key=value`. Goose-only; ignored by other frameworks. Opt-in: absent/empty → recipes byte-identical.

requirement.description: Goose requirement (default: optional). Optional non-file params receive a default; file params are coerced to required (goose forbids file defaults).

default.description: Default for optional non-file params (file params cannot have a default).

recipe_response.description: Optional Goose recipe response schema (Phase-4b goose-native). A JSON Schema ({type:object, properties, required, ...}) declaring the team's structured output; for --framework goose it is copied to the team-manifest and emitted as a `response:` block (json_schema) on the ORCHESTRATOR recipe so goose validates the final output against it. Goose-only; ignored by other frameworks. Opt-in: absent → recipes byte-identical.

recipe_retry.description: Optional Goose recipe retry config (Phase-4c goose-native). Declares self-validation for the generated Goose team: for --framework goose it is copied to the team-manifest and emitted as a `retry:` block on the ORCHESTRATOR recipe so goose re-runs until the shell `checks` pass. Safety: `max_retries` is clamped to 1-10 (default 3) and a bounded `timeout_seconds` (1-3600, default 300) is always emitted (never an unbounded retry). Requires ≥1 valid check or it is dropped. Goose-only; ignored by other frameworks. Opt-in: absent → recipes byte-identical.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-check command timeout in seconds (clamped to 1-3600; default 300).

checks.description: Shell checks defining success; ALL must pass.

command.description: Shell command; non-zero exit = check failed.

on_failure.description: Shell command run when checks fail (optional).
